Kestrel Bay Capital Ltd — AU Data Processing Addendum (Schedule: Privacy, Retention and Disclosure Controls) — Effective 15 June 2026
This schedule applies to employment records (including site health declarations), credit applications for suppliers, and client transaction instructions processed by Kestrel Bay Capital Ltd and its processors.
Personal information is handled in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and the Australian Privacy Principles; MNPI is handled under the Corporations Act and ASX continuous disclosure obligations.
Cross‑border transfer: Data‑export approval AU‑DEX‑2026‑014 authorises HR payroll metadata to Kestrel Bay Capital NZ Ltd via SFTP on 13 May 2026; only aggregate overtime metrics are exported, with government identifiers and medical notes excluded.
There is currently no active export of customer account statements or trade confirmations to overseas recipients; the 2025 pilot to Bluebridge Cloud Services Pte. Ltd. (SG) has concluded and the dataset was purged on 02 Feb 2026.
Consent withdrawal: Ms. Rowan E. Hartford withdrew direct‑marketing consent on 01 June 2026; CRM and email systems have been suppressed and a marketing‑opt‑out audit log retained for compliance.
DSAR (APP 12 access): Mr. Jamal T. Coen requested copies of his employment and payroll records on 27 May 2026; copies were provided on 31 May 2026 and identity was verified without collecting additional identifiers.
Erasure: Former contractor Dr. Narelle Quinn requested deletion of recruitment files on 05 May 2026; interview notes and psychometric reports were destroyed on 20 May 2026, subject to statutory retention of invoicing records for 7 years.
Retention: health and site‑access declarations are kept for 12 months unless an incident requires longer retention; CCTV logs roll on 30‑day cycles; trade‑related communications are retained for 5 years to meet ASIC expectations.
Data minimisation: supplier credit application forms have been redesigned to remove collection of personal bank statements; only name, position, work email, and employer address are collected for credit checks via Oakhaven Credit Pty Ltd (AU‑only gateway).
MNPI control: transaction‑related materials shared with processors exclude any not‑generally‑available price‑sensitive details; this schedule does not authorise disclosure of inside information or create a trading window.
Security and transfer safeguards: overseas recipients must contractually commit to APP‑consistent protections, encryption at rest (AES‑256), and reporting of privacy incidents within 72 hours to our Privacy Officer.
Negative assurance: no health information is used for employment decisions beyond site‑access fitness confirmations, and no TFNs are stored in analytics datasets used for headcount planning.
Retention review: quarterly audits test compliance with the above timeframes and raise tickets for deletion; all overdue deletion tickets older than Q1‑2026 have been closed.
Contact: Privacy Officer, Ms. Tahlia K. Merriweather, privacy@kestrelbay.capital, Level 23, 18 Kookaburra Terrace, North Hanfield VIC, Australia; complaints may be escalated to the OAIC if unresolved.
Requests and objections: submit access or correction requests to dsar.au@kestrelbay.capital; consent may be withdrawn at any time for marketing; response targets are 30 days (standard) and 5 business days for opt‑outs.
